Introduction to Lubbock County

Arrows in Lubbock County

Arrow #38: Lubbock

More Details for Arrow #38

Coordinates: 33.5854936, -101.8241339

Installed: July 12, 2012

Dedicated: September 16, 2016

Marker Text:

Comanche hunters traded bison meat for N.M. Indian corn carted to Yellow House Canyon by Comancheros 1786-1875 Nomadic Indians gathered for food plants native to Yellow House Canyon hackberry * plum * grape * cactus fruit

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #85: Lubbock

More Details for Arrow #85

Coordinates: 33.5910245, -101.8839993

Installed: October 3, 2016

Dedicated: October 8, 2016

Marker Text:

None

No image available for this arrow.

Welcome to the Quanah Parker Trail!

Explore the historical arrow markers commemorating Quanah Parker and the Comanche people across the Texas Plains Trail Region. Use the map on the homepage or navigate via the menu to learn more about this effort.
